concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a prominent DEQ (Department of Environmental Quality) logo at the bottom left of page 1. The HTML output does not contain any image element corresponding to this logo. The images present in the HTML correspond to the top State Seal, a duplicated snippet of text, and the signature on page 2. The meaningful agency logo at the footer of page 1 is completely absent.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: DEQ logo at the bottom left of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: Missing element for the DEQ logo.

concerns.analysis:
The HTML output contains an image that visually duplicates text already extracted in the document body. The numbered list is properly extracted as an `
    ` element, but an `` element is included shortly after it, whose alt text explicitly describes it as a snippet of the document containing the numbered list requirements about sediment basin baffles and contacting well owners (items 3 and 4). This means the user would see the text once as structured text and again as a cropped image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items 3 and 4 on page 1.
concerns.output_quote:
...
  1. Show the sediment basin baffle placement...
  2. Please re-contact the 20 well owners...
...

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 3 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (3 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...Department of Environmental Quality, featuring the"; p1: "...garding sediment basin baffles and contacting well"; p2: "...faint blue signature above the printed name Judith"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=3 (truncated=3, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.
The source PDF uses underline emphasis for 'Certified Mail' and 'Return Receipt Requested' on page 1, and 'Certified Mail' on page 2. This underlining is entirely lost in the extraction JSON.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
